sn46.5:1.2.pli: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn46.5:1.2.eng: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha … and asked him,

sn46.5:1.4.eng: How are the awakening factors defined?”

sn46.5:1.5.eng: “Mendicant, they’re called awakening factors because they lead to awakening.

sn46.5:1.6.pli: Idha, bhikkhu, satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe…
sn46.5:1.6.eng: A mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,

sn46.5:1.7.pli: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn46.5:1.7.eng: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.

sn46.5:1.8.pli: Tassime satta bojjhaṅge bhāvayato kāmāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati, bhavāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati, avijjāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati.
sn46.5:1.8.eng: As they develop the seven awakening factors, their mind is freed from the defilements of sensuality, desire to be reborn, and ignorance.

sn46.5:1.9.eng: When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.

sn46.5:1.10.pli: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.
sn46.5:1.10.eng: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’

sn46.5:1.11.eng: They’re called awakening factors because they lead to awakening.”

sn46.50:1.1.pli: “Bāhiraṁ, bhikkhave, aṅganti karitvā nāññaṁ ekaṅgampi samanupassāmi sattannaṁ bojjhaṅgānaṁ uppādāya, yathayidaṁ—bhikkhave, kalyāṇamittatā.
sn46.50:1.1.eng: “Taking into account exterior factors, mendicants, I do not see a single one that gives rise to the seven awakening factors like good friendship. …”

sn46.50:1.2.pli: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—satta bojjhaṅge bhāvessati, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkarissati.

sn46.50:1.3.pli: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto satta bojjhaṅge bhāveti, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkaroti?

sn46.50:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ …pe…

sn46.50:1.5.pli: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.

sn46.50:1.6.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto satta bojjhaṅge bhāveti, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkarotī”ti.

sn46.51:1.2.pli: “Pañcannañca, bhikkhave, nīvaraṇānaṁ sattannañca bojjhaṅgānaṁ āhārañca anāhārañca desessāmi;
sn46.51:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, I will teach you what fuels and what starves the five hindrances and the seven awakening factors.

sn46.51:1.4.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā kāmacchandassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā kāmacchandassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya?
sn46.51:1.4.eng: And what fuels the arising of sensual desire, or, when it has arisen, makes it increase and grow?

sn46.51:1.5.eng: There is the feature of beauty.

sn46.51:1.6.eng: Frequent irrational application of mind to that

sn46.51:1.7.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā kāmacchandassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā kāmacchandassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya.
sn46.51:1.7.eng: fuels the arising of sensual desire, or, when it has arisen, makes it increase and grow.

sn46.51:2.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā byāpādassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā byāpādassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya?
sn46.51:2.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of ill will, or, when it has arisen, makes it increase and grow?

sn46.51:2.2.eng: There is the feature of harshness.

sn46.51:2.3.eng: Frequent irrational application of mind to that

sn46.51:2.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā byāpādassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā byāpādassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya.
sn46.51:2.4.eng: fuels the arising of ill will, or, when it has arisen, makes it increase and grow.

sn46.51:3.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā thinamiddhassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā thinamiddhassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya?
sn46.51:3.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of dullness and drowsiness, or, when they have arisen, makes them increase and grow?

sn46.51:3.2.pli: Atthi, bhikkhave, arati tandi vijambhitā bhattasammado cetaso ca līnattaṁ.
sn46.51:3.2.eng: There is discontent, sloth, yawning, sleepiness after eating, and mental sluggishness.

sn46.51:3.3.eng: Frequent irrational application of mind to that

sn46.51:3.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā thinamiddhassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā thinamiddhassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya.
sn46.51:3.4.eng: fuels the arising of dullness and drowsiness, or, when they have arisen, makes them increase and grow.

sn46.51:4.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā uddhaccakukkuccassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā uddhaccakukkuccassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya?
sn46.51:4.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of restlessness and remorse, or, when they have arisen, makes them increase and grow?

sn46.51:4.3.eng: Frequent irrational application of mind to that

sn46.51:4.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā uddhaccakukkuccassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā uddhaccakukkuccassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya.
sn46.51:4.4.eng: fuels the arising of restlessness and remorse, or, when they have arisen, makes them increase and grow.

sn46.51:5.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannāya vā vicikicchāya uppādāya, uppannāya vā vicikicchāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya?
sn46.51:5.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of doubt, or, when it has arisen, makes it increase and grow?

sn46.51:5.2.eng: There are things that are grounds for doubt.

sn46.51:5.3.eng: Frequent irrational application of mind to them

sn46.51:5.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannāya vā vicikicchāya uppādāya, uppannāya vā vicikicchāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya.
sn46.51:5.4.eng: fuels the arising of doubt, or, when it has arisen, makes it increase and grow.

sn46.51:6.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā satisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā satisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:6.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of the awakening factor of mindfulness, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it?

sn46.51:6.2.eng: There are things that are grounds for the awakening factor of mindfulness.

sn46.51:6.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to them

sn46.51:6.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā satisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā satisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:6.4.eng: fuels the arising of the awakening factor of mindfulness, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it.

sn46.51:7.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:7.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of the awakening factor of investigation of principles, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it?

sn46.51:7.2.pli: Atthi, bhikkhave, kusalākusalā dhammā sāvajjānavajjā dhammā hīnapaṇītā dhammā kaṇhasukkasappaṭibhāgā dhammā.
sn46.51:7.2.eng: There are qualities that are skillful and unskillful, blameworthy and blameless, inferior and superior, and those on the side of dark and bright.

sn46.51:7.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to them

sn46.51:7.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:7.4.eng: fuels the arising of the awakening factor of investigation of principles, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it.

sn46.51:8.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:8.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of the awakening factor of energy, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it?

sn46.51:8.2.eng: There are the elements of initiative, persistence, and exertion.

sn46.51:8.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to them

sn46.51:8.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:8.4.eng: fuels the arising of the awakening factor of energy, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it.

sn46.51:9.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā pītisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā pītisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:9.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of the awakening factor of rapture, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it?

sn46.51:9.2.eng: There are things that are grounds for the awakening factor of rapture.

sn46.51:9.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to them

sn46.51:9.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā pītisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā pītisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:9.4.eng: fuels the arising of the awakening factor of rapture, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it.

sn46.51:10.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:10.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of the awakening factor of tranquility, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it?

sn46.51:10.2.eng: There is tranquility of the body and of the mind.

sn46.51:10.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to that

sn46.51:10.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:10.4.eng: fuels the arising of the awakening factor of tranquility, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it.

sn46.51:11.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā samādhisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā samādhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:11.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of the awakening factor of immersion, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it?

sn46.51:11.2.eng: There are things that are the basis of serenity and freedom from distraction.

sn46.51:11.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to them

sn46.51:11.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā samādhisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā samādhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:11.4.eng: fuels the arising of the awakening factor of immersion, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it.

sn46.51:12.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, āhāro anuppannassa vā upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:12.1.eng: And what fuels the arising of the awakening factor of equanimity, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it?

sn46.51:12.2.eng: There are things that are grounds for the awakening factor of equanimity.

sn46.51:12.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to them

sn46.51:12.4.pli: ayamāhāro anuppannassa vā upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:12.4.eng: fuels the arising of the awakening factor of equanimity, or, when it has arisen, fully develops it.

sn46.51:13.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā kāmacchandassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā kāmacchandassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya?
sn46.51:13.1.eng: And what starves the arising of sensual desire, or, when it has arisen, starves its increase and growth?

sn46.51:13.2.eng: There is the feature of ugliness.

sn46.51:13.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to that

sn46.51:13.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā kāmacchandassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā kāmacchandassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya.
sn46.51:13.4.eng: starves the arising of sensual desire, or, when it has arisen, starves its increase and growth.

sn46.51:14.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā byāpādassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā byāpādassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya?
sn46.51:14.1.eng: And what starves the arising of ill will, or, when it has arisen, starves its increase and growth?

sn46.51:14.2.eng: There is the heart’s release by love.

sn46.51:14.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to that

sn46.51:14.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā byāpādassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā byāpādassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya.
sn46.51:14.4.eng: starves the arising of ill will, or, when it has arisen, starves its increase and growth.

sn46.51:15.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā thinamiddhassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā thinamiddhassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya?
sn46.51:15.1.eng: And what starves the arising of dullness and drowsiness, or, when they have arisen, starves their increase and growth?

sn46.51:15.2.eng: There are the elements of initiative, persistence, and exertion.

sn46.51:15.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to them

sn46.51:15.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā thinamiddhassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā thinamiddhassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya.
sn46.51:15.4.eng: starves the arising of dullness and drowsiness, or, when they have arisen, starves their increase and growth.

sn46.51:16.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā uddhaccakukkuccassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā uddhaccakukkuccassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya?
sn46.51:16.1.eng: And what starves the arising of restlessness and remorse, or, when they have arisen, starves their increase and growth?

sn46.51:16.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to that

sn46.51:16.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā uddhaccakukkuccassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā uddhaccakukkuccassa bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya.
sn46.51:16.4.eng: starves the arising of restlessness and remorse, or, when they have arisen, starves their increase and growth.

sn46.51:17.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannāya vā vicikicchāya uppādāya, uppannāya vā vicikicchāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya?
sn46.51:17.1.eng: And what starves the arising of doubt, or, when it has arisen, starves its increase and growth?

sn46.51:17.2.pli: Atthi, bhikkhave, kusalākusalā dhammā sāvajjānavajjā dhammā hīnapaṇītā dhammā kaṇhasukkasappaṭibhāgā dhammā.
sn46.51:17.2.eng: There are qualities that are skillful and unskillful, blameworthy and blameless, inferior and superior, and those on the side of dark and bright.

sn46.51:17.3.eng: Frequent rational application of mind to them

sn46.51:17.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannāya vā vicikicchāya uppādāya, uppannāya vā vicikicchāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya.
sn46.51:17.4.eng: starves the arising of doubt, or, when it has arisen, starves its increase and growth.

sn46.51:18.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā satisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā satisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:18.1.eng: And what starves the arising of the awakening factor of mindfulness, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development?

sn46.51:18.2.eng: There are things that are grounds for the awakening factor of mindfulness.

sn46.51:18.3.eng: Not frequently applying the mind to that

sn46.51:18.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā satisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā satisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:18.4.eng: starves the arising of the awakening factor of mindfulness, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development.

sn46.51:19.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:19.1.eng: And what starves the arising of the awakening factor of investigation of principles, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development?

sn46.51:19.2.pli: Atthi, bhikkhave, kusalākusalā dhammā sāvajjānavajjā dhammā hīnapaṇītā dhammā kaṇhasukkasappaṭibhāgā dhammā.
sn46.51:19.2.eng: There are qualities that are skillful and unskillful, blameworthy and blameless, inferior and superior, and those on the side of dark and bright.

sn46.51:19.3.eng: Not frequently applying the mind to that

sn46.51:19.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:19.4.eng: starves the arising of the awakening factor of investigation of principles, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development.

sn46.51:20.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:20.1.eng: And what starves the arising of the awakening factor of energy, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development?

sn46.51:20.2.eng: There are the elements of initiative, persistence, and exertion.

sn46.51:20.3.eng: Not frequently applying the mind to that

sn46.51:20.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:20.4.eng: starves the arising of the awakening factor of energy, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development.

sn46.51:21.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā pītisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā pītisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:21.1.eng: And what starves the arising of the awakening factor of rapture, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development?

sn46.51:21.2.eng: There are things that are grounds for the awakening factor of rapture.

sn46.51:21.3.eng: Not frequently applying the mind to that

sn46.51:21.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā pītisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā pītisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:21.4.eng: starves the arising of the awakening factor of rapture, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development.

sn46.51:22.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:22.1.eng: And what starves the arising of the awakening factor of tranquility, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development?

sn46.51:22.2.eng: There is tranquility of the body and of the mind.

sn46.51:22.3.eng: Not frequently applying the mind to that

sn46.51:22.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:22.4.eng: starves the arising of the awakening factor of tranquility, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development.

sn46.51:23.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā samādhisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā samādhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:23.1.eng: And what starves the arising of the awakening factor of immersion, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development?

sn46.51:23.2.eng: There are things that are the basis of serenity and freedom from distraction.

sn46.51:23.3.eng: Not frequently applying the mind to that

sn46.51:23.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā samādhisambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā samādhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā.
sn46.51:23.4.eng: starves the arising of the awakening factor of immersion, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development.

sn46.51:24.1.pli: Ko ca, bhikkhave, anāhāro anuppannassa vā upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā?
sn46.51:24.1.eng: And what starves the arising of the awakening factor of equanimity, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development?

sn46.51:24.2.eng: There are things that are grounds for the awakening factor of equanimity.

sn46.51:24.3.eng: Not frequently applying the mind to that

sn46.51:24.4.pli: ayamanāhāro anuppannassa vā upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa uppādāya, uppannassa vā upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūriyā”ti.
sn46.51:24.4.eng: starves the arising of the awakening factor of equanimity, or, when it has arisen, starves its full development.”

sn46.52:1.1.pli: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pavisiṁsu.
sn46.52:1.1.eng: Then several mendicants robed up in the morning and, taking their bowls and robes, entered Sāvatthī for alms.

sn46.52:1.3.eng: “It’s too early to wander for alms in Sāvatthī.

sn46.52:1.4.pli: Yannūna mayaṁ yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkameyyāmā”ti.
sn46.52:1.4.eng: Why don’t we visit the monastery of the wanderers of other religions?”

sn46.52:2.1.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā tehi aññatitthiyehi paribbājakehi saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
sn46.52:2.1.eng: Then they went to the monastery of the wanderers of other religions and exchanged greetings with the wanderers there.

sn46.52:2.2.pli: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho te bhikkhū aññatitthiyā paribbājakā etadavocuṁ:
sn46.52:2.2.eng: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side. The wanderers said to them:

sn46.52:3.1.pli: “Samaṇo, āvuso, gotamo sāvakānaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
sn46.52:3.1.eng: “Reverends, the ascetic Gotama teaches his disciples like this:

sn46.52:3.2.pli: ‘etha tumhe, bhikkhave, pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe satta bojjhaṅge yathābhūtaṁ bhāvethā’ti.
sn46.52:3.2.eng: ‘Mendicants, please give up the five hindrances—corruptions of the heart that weaken wisdom—and truly develop the seven awakening factors.’

sn46.52:3.3.pli: Mayampi kho, āvuso, sāvakānaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ desema:

sn46.52:3.4.pli: ‘etha tumhe, āvuso, pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe satta bojjhaṅge yathābhūtaṁ bhāvethā’ti.
sn46.52:3.4.eng: ‘Reverends, please give up the five hindrances—corruptions of the heart that weaken wisdom—and truly develop the seven awakening factors.’

sn46.52:3.5.pli: Idha no, āvuso, ko viseso, ko adhippayāso, kiṁ nānākaraṇaṁ samaṇassa vā gotamassa amhākaṁ vā, yadidaṁ—dhammadesanāya vā dhammadesanaṁ, anusāsaniyā vā anusāsanin”ti?
sn46.52:3.5.eng: What, then, is the difference between the ascetic Gotama’s teaching and instruction and ours?”

sn46.52:4.1.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ bhāsitaṁ neva abhinandiṁsu nappaṭikkosiṁsu;
sn46.52:4.1.eng: Those mendicants neither approved nor rejected that statement of the wanderers of other religions.

sn46.52:4.2.eng: They got up from their seat, thinking:

sn46.52:4.3.eng: “We will learn the meaning of this statement from the Buddha himself.”

sn46.52:4.4.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
sn46.52:4.4.eng: Then, after the meal, when they returned from almsround, they went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.

sn46.52:5.1.pli: “Idha mayaṁ, bhante, pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pavisimha.

sn46.52:5.4.pli: yannūna mayaṁ yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkameyyāmā’ti.

sn46.52:5.5.pli: Atha kho mayaṁ, bhante, yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkamimha; upasaṅkamitvā tehi aññatitthiyehi paribbājakehi saddhiṁ sammodimha.

sn46.52:5.6.pli: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdimha. Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho amhe, bhante, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā etadavocuṁ:

sn46.52:6.1.pli: ‘Samaṇo, āvuso, gotamo sāvakānaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ deseti “etha tumhe, bhikkhave, pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe satta bojjhaṅge yathābhūtaṁ bhāvethā”ti.

sn46.52:6.2.pli: Mayampi kho, āvuso, sāvakānaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ desema:

sn46.52:6.3.pli: “etha tumhe, āvuso, pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe satta bojjhaṅge yathābhūtaṁ bhāvethā”ti.

sn46.52:6.4.pli: Idha no, āvuso, ko viseso, ko adhippayāso, kiṁ nānākaraṇaṁ samaṇassa vā gotamassa amhākaṁ vā, yadidaṁ—dhammadesanāya vā dhammadesanaṁ, anusāsaniyā vā anusāsanin’ti?

sn46.52:7.1.pli: Atha kho mayaṁ, bhante, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ bhāsitaṁ neva abhinandimha nappaṭikkosimha, anabhinanditvā appaṭikkositvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamimha:

sn46.52:8.1.eng: “Mendicants, when wanderers of other religions say this, you should say to them:

sn46.52:8.2.pli: ‘atthi panāvuso, pariyāyo, yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma pañca nīvaraṇā dasa honti, satta bojjhaṅgā catuddasā’ti.
sn46.52:8.2.eng: ‘But reverends, is there a way in which the five hindrances become ten and the seven awakening factors become fourteen?’

sn46.52:8.3.pli: Evaṁ puṭṭhā, bhikkhave, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā na ceva sampāyissanti, uttariñca vighātaṁ āpajjissanti.
sn46.52:8.3.eng: Questioned like this, the wanderers of other religions would be stumped, and, in addition, would get frustrated.

sn46.52:8.5.eng: Because they’re out of their element.

sn46.52:8.6.pli: Nāhaṁ taṁ, bhikkhave, passāmi sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya, yo imesaṁ pañhānaṁ veyyākaraṇena cittaṁ ārādheyya, aññatra tathāgatena vā tathāgatasāvakena vā ito vā pana sutvā.
sn46.52:8.6.eng: I don’t see anyone in this world—with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans—who could provide a satisfying answer to these questions except for the Realized One or his disciple or someone who has heard it from them.

sn46.52:9.1.pli: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, pariyāyo, yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma pañca nīvaraṇā dasa honti?
sn46.52:9.1.eng: And what is the way in which the five hindrances become ten?

sn46.52:9.2.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ kāmacchando tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ, yadapi bahiddhā kāmacchando tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ.
sn46.52:9.2.eng: Sensual desire for what is internal is a hindrance; and sensual desire for what is external is also a hindrance.

sn46.52:9.3.pli: ‘Kāmacchandanīvaraṇan’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:9.3.eng: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the hindrance of sensual desire’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:9.4.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ byāpādo tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ, yadapi bahiddhā byāpādo tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ.
sn46.52:9.4.eng: Ill will for what is internal is a hindrance; and ill will for what is external is also a hindrance.

sn46.52:9.5.pli: ‘Byāpādanīvaraṇan’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:9.5.eng: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the hindrance of ill will’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:9.6.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, thinaṁ tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ, yadapi middhaṁ tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ.
sn46.52:9.6.eng: Dullness is a hindrance; and drowsiness is also a hindrance.

sn46.52:9.7.pli: ‘Thinamiddhanīvaraṇan’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:9.7.eng: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the hindrance of dullness and drowsiness’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:9.8.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, uddhaccaṁ tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ, yadapi kukkuccaṁ tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ.
sn46.52:9.8.eng: Restlessness is a hindrance; and remorse is also a hindrance.

sn46.52:9.9.pli: ‘Uddhaccakukkuccanīvaraṇan’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:9.9.eng: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the hindrance of restlessness and remorse’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:9.10.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ dhammesu vicikicchā tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ, yadapi bahiddhā dhammesu vicikicchā tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ.
sn46.52:9.10.eng: Doubt about internal things is a hindrance; and doubt about external things is also a hindrance.

sn46.52:9.11.pli: ‘Vicikicchānīvaraṇan’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:9.11.eng: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the hindrance of doubt’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:9.12.pli: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pariyāyo, yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma pañca nīvaraṇā dasa honti.
sn46.52:9.12.eng: This is the way in which the five hindrances become ten.

sn46.52:10.1.pli: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, pariyāyo, yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma satta bojjhaṅgā catuddasa honti?
sn46.52:10.1.eng: And what is the way in which the seven awakening factors become fourteen?

sn46.52:10.2.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ dhammesu sati tadapi satisambojjhaṅgo, yadapi bahiddhā dhammesu sati tadapi satisambojjhaṅgo.
sn46.52:10.2.eng: Mindfulness of internal things is the awakening factor of mindfulness; and mindfulness of external things is also the awakening factor of mindfulness.

sn46.52:10.3.pli: ‘Satisambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:10.3.eng: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of mindfulness’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:11.1.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ dhammesu paññāya pavicinati pavicarati parivīmaṁsamāpajjati tadapi dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo, yadapi bahiddhā dhammesu paññāya pavicinati pavicarati parivīmaṁsamāpajjati tadapi dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo.
sn46.52:11.1.eng: Investigating, exploring, and inquiring into internal things with wisdom is the awakening factor of investigation of principles; and investigating, exploring, and inquiring into external things with wisdom is also the awakening factor of investigation of principles.

sn46.52:11.2.pli: ‘Dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:11.2.eng: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of investigation of principles’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:12.1.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, kāyikaṁ vīriyaṁ tadapi vīriyasambojjhaṅgo, yadapi cetasikaṁ vīriyaṁ tadapi vīriyasambojjhaṅgo.
sn46.52:12.1.eng: Physical energy is the awakening factor of energy; and mental energy is also the awakening factor of energy.

sn46.52:12.2.pli: ‘Vīriyasambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:12.2.eng: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of energy’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:13.1.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, savitakkasavicārā pīti tadapi pītisambojjhaṅgo, yadapi avitakkaavicārā pīti tadapi pītisambojjhaṅgo.
sn46.52:13.1.eng: Rapture while placing the mind and keeping it connected is the awakening factor of rapture; and rapture without placing the mind and keeping it connected is also the awakening factor of rapture.

sn46.52:13.2.pli: ‘Pītisambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:13.2.eng: In this way what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of rapture’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:14.1.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, kāyappassaddhi tadapi passaddhisambojjhaṅgo, yadapi cittappassaddhi tadapi passaddhisambojjhaṅgo.
sn46.52:14.1.eng: Physical tranquility is the awakening factor of tranquility; and mental tranquility is also the awakening factor of tranquility.

sn46.52:14.2.pli: ‘Passaddhisambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:14.2.eng: In this way what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of tranquility’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:15.1.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, savitakko savicāro samādhi tadapi samādhisambojjhaṅgo, yadapi avitakkaavicāro samādhi tadapi samādhisambojjhaṅgo.
sn46.52:15.1.eng: Immersion while placing the mind and keeping it connected is the awakening factor of immersion; and immersion without placing the mind and keeping it connected is also the awakening factor of immersion.

sn46.52:15.2.pli: ‘Samādhisambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:15.2.eng: In this way what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of immersion’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:16.1.pli: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ dhammesu upekkhā tadapi upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo, yadapi bahiddhā dhammesu upekkhā tadapi upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo.
sn46.52:16.1.eng: Equanimity for internal things is the awakening factor of equanimity; and equanimity for external things is also the awakening factor of equanimity.

sn46.52:16.2.pli: ‘Upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
sn46.52:16.2.eng: In this way what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of equanimity’ becomes twofold.

sn46.52:16.3.pli: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pariyāyo, yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma satta bojjhaṅgā catuddasā”ti.
sn46.52:16.3.eng: This is the way in which the seven awakening factors become fourteen.”
